body_txt: This trope is when mustaches represent authority, military service, and obsessive professionalism. Mustaches are seen as an emblem of discipline and orderliness, representing the wearer's diligence and uniformity in their duties. In certain institutions, mustaches are enforced as a mandatory grooming standard, especially in military services. Historically, soldiers and officers were required to grow mustaches and follow grooming regulations as a display of self-discipline. Mustaches function as a cultural marker of a military-focused society . In this culture, mustaches are compulsory to reinforce a warrior ethos and represent lifelong loyalty to the empire. Mustachioed soldiers have been trained in the ins and outs of warfare and are dedicated to their culture because they're raised to serve the best interests of their empire. Mustaches imply the wearer is adhering to the standards set by a larger system that prioritizes their military proficiency. It's a visual cue of someone strong, dependable, and disciplined While the trope is most famously associated with military men, it has extended into other professions like police officers , who wear mustaches to be seen as approachable and reliable by the public. Lampshade mustaches are nicknamed "Cop 'Stache" or "Military Mustache" because of this association. Although the precise origin is unclear, the history of the mustache within the military is complicated. It's believed to have originated in Hungary in the 17th century, as they believed it was part of their national identity and made them appear intimidating to their foes. There are also accounts of it originating in France in the 18th century. The French army was also required to wear mustaches, and each unit would have its own style of facial hair to differentiate itself. Finally, within The British Empire , mustaches were mandatory from 1860 to 1916. British soldiers would face disciplinary action if they were to shave their facial hair. The military association with mustaches is often thought to have been reinforced and formalized, in part, through Britain’s global imperial presence. Regular and attentive grooming of facial hair signifies the capacity for self-management and willingness to adhere to regulations, even those that may appear minor or insignificant. It conveys a sense of calmness and composure in challenging or dangerous situations. A well-maintained mustache serves as a visual indicator that the individual is capable of managing stress effectively, can be relied upon during crises, and demonstrates competence to both civilians and fellow soldiers. Conversely, an unkempt mustache may suggest that the individual is negligent, unreliable, and unable to manage even the most basic responsibilities, such as personal grooming. In leadership roles, grooming reflects not only personal pride but also the standard expected of those they command. On a leader, a disheveled mustache reflects hopelessness or their waning resolve , which can lower the morale of subordinates. Although they may not have served in the military, a mustache can still convey that a character is dedicated to their work, industrious, and invested in maintaining a strong public image. For civilians or working-class individuals, a neatly trimmed mustache functions as a visual indicator of reliability and self-discipline, reflecting good hygiene, integrity, and respect for one’s profession. It signals that the individual is dependable and trustworthy to clients and employers, demonstrating that they take their work seriously, care about doing it well, and maintain enough self-respect to avoid half-hearted or careless efforts. Conversely, an unkempt mustache suggests negligence in personal grooming, implying broader carelessness and undermining perceptions of competence and reliability. Mustaches were discontinued in the military and the workplace because thick mustaches and beards prevented gas masks from being completely sealed during the First World War . Under OSHA requirements , mustaches must be neatly trimmed so masks can be sealed completely. Mustaches (chevron mustaches in particular) slowly became old-fashioned, with Porn Stache being a common insult and derogatory term for the mustache. Not to be confused with Manly Facial Hair because this refers to styles of facial hair (beards and mustaches) associated with masculinity or showing that a character is manly. "'Stache Of Duty" is about how the mustache evokes authority, diligence, duty, honor, and reliability. It can go with Manly Facial Hair and Good Hair, Evil Hair . Not to be confused with Hereditary Hairstyle . Can go with Horseshoe Mustache of Toughness if the character sports a horseshoe mustache and is clearly portrayed as tough. Can go with Upper Class 'Stache if the character has a mustache with curled or waxed tips and is attempting to present himself as a refined, upper-class gentleman. Can go with Dapper Pencil Mustache if the character wears a pencil mustache and is depicted as suave and dapper. Can go with Godwin's Law of Facial Hair if the character has a toothbrush mustache and is portrayed as an authoritarian dictator, or is perceived as fascistic or dictatorial by others due to their behavior. Can go with Martial Fu Manchu Mustache if the Fu Manchu mustache is worn by Asian warriors and is used to evoke associations with Asian martial or military culture.
